title: xanthophyllum pachycarpon
intro: Xanthophyllum pachycarpon, also known as the 'thorny xanthophyllum', is a plant species belonging to the family Polygalaceae. It is primarily found in the tropical regions of Southeast Asia. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its leaves, which contain flavonoids and alkaloids, and have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The leaves can be prepared as a decoction or infusion, and have been used in traditional herbal medicine to promote health and well-being.
